slides.text: The cheaper challenger just out-coded the premium name on a benchmark — for about half the price. Real, or just a test?
slides.image_prompt: A vertical viral Instagram news post. A full frame high quality photograph, cropped to the top ~70% of the frame, of a head-to-head versus composition — two laptops on a desk angled toward each other, the left screen glowing cool dark with a generic coding-assistant interface mid-task, the right screen glowing warm cream with a different generic coding-assistant interface mid-task, a subtle sense of a face-off between them; believable generic code editors, neither a pixel-copy of any real product's actual screen; real device materials and screen glow, shallow depth of field. Along the top edge of the photo, lay the connected reference image Straw_Motion_Logo horizontally, small and subtle in scale — no wider than roughly a third of the frame width — so it stays a quiet brand mark and never competes with the headline or stat callout for attention. Render the logo exactly as provided in the reference image: do not change its shape, proportions, or color in any way, and do not restyle it to match the scene. A soft dark vignette sits behind it for legibility against the photo. Below the photo, filling the bottom ~30% of the frame, is a solid white rounded card sitting on top of the image like a native app UI panel, full width, edge to edge. Inside the card, top-left, is a pill-shaped badge — a fully rounded, stadium-shaped outline with a thin black border and a white fill — containing the text "Unhypped News" centered inside it, set in Inter font, black text. Render the text inside the pill exactly as "Unhypped News" — capital U, capital N, every other letter lowercase. Never render it in all-caps/uppercase lettering, no matter the surrounding typography style. Positioned next to the pill are two tiny real product logos in a row — the xAI Grok logo and the Anthropic Claude logo — the two companies in this comparison. Below that, in large bold black display type, is the stat callout "Cheaper. And better?", and beneath it, in smaller near-black sentence-case text, the supporting line "The cheaper challenger just out-coded the premium name on a benchmark — for about half the price. Real, or just a test?" All text on the card — inside the "Unhypped News" pill, the stat callout, and the supporting line — is set in Inter. Clean editorial social media news page layout. Photorealistic, crisp bold typography overlay for the photo, clean flat UI-card typography for the card.

slides.text: Smart move: trial the challenger on real work before you switch anything. One benchmark isn't a migration plan.
